description: Redis used as a datastore with RDB-only persistence loses every write since the last snapshot on a crash.
body: Failure mode: Redis holds data the app cannot reconstruct but is configured with only periodic RDB snapshots; a crash between snapshots discards minutes of writes, and the app reads a stale dataset after restart. Detection signals: missing recent writes after a Redis restart; only save points configured, AOF disabled; the loss window matches the snapshot interval. Do not assume Redis is durable by default. Safer fallback: enable AOF (appendfsync everysec or always) for datastore roles, optionally with RDB for fast restore, and run a kill-restart test to confirm the loss window is acceptable.
